explicit_text: I was born in the town of Prato on March 22, 1895. My family had a strong connection to this place because my grandfather and his brother Giulio founded a woolen factory here. The factory grew over time, first using water power from the nearby Bisenzio river and later building a bigger factory closer to the plain of Prato when electricity became available. Prato was not just my birthplace but also the center of my family's business and life.

explicit_text: My father, Alfredo Forti, was a very gifted man who took over our family woolen factory after my grandfather and his brother Giulio. He was involved in the business from a young age, though he also loved literature and poetry, often teaching us Italian classics during vacations. Under his leadership, the factory expanded, including building a larger facility closer to Prato when electricity became available. Alfredo was not only a businessman but also a cultured man who valued education and the arts.

She came from a family with notable members, including a brother who became a psychiatrist influenced by Sigmund Freud's emerging ideas. Her background and family connections enriched our household and influenced our upbringing.

explicit_text: Giulio Forti was my grandfather's brother and co-founder of the woolen factory in Prato. Together, they built a successful business producing beautiful plaids that were sent worldwide, especially to England. Giulio played a crucial role in establishing the factory near the Bisenzio river, which powered the machines before electricity was available. His vision and skill helped make our family well-known in the textile industry.

explicit_text: The Bisenzio is the river near which my grandfather and Giulio Forti built their original woolen factory in Prato. Since there was no electricity at the time, the factory relied on the river's water power to run the machines. I remember as a child being both fascinated and frightened by the machines powered by the Bisenzio. This river was essential to the early success of our family business.
